title: Struggling with MT5 trading bots (Python vs MQL5) — need advice on building a profitable strategy
author: Massive-Pomelo-4470
selftext:
Ps. im a beginner tradse using help of ai for my codes . i’m building trading bots in Python (MT5 API) and also testing MQL5, but I keep running into issues:
BUY and SELL signals from different logic at the same time
Exit signals are inconsistent or missing
MT5 errors like “invalid volume” / “unsupported filling mode”
Results change completely depending on indicators (RSI, ATR, momentum, etc.)
I’m starting to think the issue is strategy design, not coding.
So I want to ask:
Is MQL5 better than Python for reliable MT5 execution?
What actually makes a trading bot profitable long-term?
Do successful bots avoid complex “prediction/AI” and just use simple rules?
How do people avoid conflicting signals from multiple indicators?
Any advice from people who’ve built working bots would help a lot.

comments.author: TradeReign
comments.body:
Maybe your bot is too complicated? Not sure because I don't use MT5. I used ninjatrader and c# and some AI help to code my strategy. It's like 50k lines of code though, so it's faily large with 100's of options and a few different modes. I think the best plan is to have a strategy that can be configured in a simple or complex way via parameters. Mine is more a breakout strategy combined with mean reversion depending on volatility.

I use indicators as well for the breakouts and use a scoring system to grade setups. Mine is simple in that it's only one active trade at a time and I think that helps simplify it. It's basically simple rules though that I configure, no AI prediction or anything. I think what makes a bot profitable long term is just lots of rules that stack the odds in your favor, then let the edge play out over many trades.

replies.author: Massive-Pomelo-4470
replies.body: Thankyou i do appreciate your input on this. i definitely believe that too many logics could be the issue. Ima try your recommendations and see

comments.author: eggrally
comments.body: Keep vibe coding until Ai gets it right
